sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Good, clicking the leftmost green block at (33, 15) worked - it transformed that green block (25 cells changed, just that one block). The board changed but no game_over. Let me check the current state to see what changed.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed after clicking leftmost green block
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

print("Current green blocks (N):")
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
        print(f"  rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}, pixels={n['pixels']}")

print("\nPlayer (b):")
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] == 'b':
        rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
        print(f"  rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")

print("\nPurple blocks (p):")
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] == 'p':
        rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
        print(f"  rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}, pixels={n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I can see what happened. The leftmost green block in the bottom row (rows 31-35, cols 13-17) is now gone. Before there were 7 green blocks in the bottom row (rows 31-35), now there are only 6 (missing the one at cols 13-17).
So clicking a green block clears it. The green blocks are:
- Top row (rows 25-29): 7 blocks at cols 13-17, 19-23, 25-29, 31-35, 37-41, 43-47, 49-53
- Bottom row (rows 31-35): 6 blocks at cols 19-23, 25-29, 31-35, 37-41, 43-47, 49-53 (missing cols 13-17)
The player is at rows 37-40, cols 20-21.
The purple blocks are at rows 7-10, cols 14-16, 20-22, 26-28.
So the goal is likely to clear all green blocks to reach the purple blocks. But I need to be careful about which blocks to click and in what order.
Previously, clicking the same block twice caused game_over. So I should only click each block once.
Let me continue clearing green blocks. I'll click them one by one, starting from the left and working right.
The remaining green blocks in the bottom row are at cols 19-23, 25-29, 31-35, 37-41, 43-47, 49-53.
The green blocks in the top row are at cols 13-17, 19-23, 25-29, 31-35, 37-41, 43-47, 49-53.
Let me click the next green block in the bottom row (cols 19-23).

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Bottom row green block at cols 19-23, rows 31-35
# Click it to clear
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 33, 'col': 21}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
